name: Cucumber
description: Cucumber — BDD test automation framework using Gherkin syntax to connect business requirements with executable tests.

seo.title: Cucumber — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Cucumber: Cucumber — BDD test automation framework using Gherkin syntax to connect business requirements with executable tests.

when_to_use: Executable business requirements with Gherkin scenarios
Shared feature files between devs, QA, and product
BDD test automation across Java, Ruby, and JS stacks
Living documentation from passing acceptance tests

pros: Shared Gherkin language between QA, devs and product
Encourages thinking in user scenarios, not methods
cons: Business folks rarely read or write the features
Step definition regex becomes a maintenance burden
Adds a translation layer on top of normal tests
Slower feedback than plain unit tests
review.verdict: Great when teams truly practice BDD, mostly overhead when they do not.
review.best_for: Teams with real collaborative three-amigos workflow on specifications
review.not_for: Dev-only shops where features end up authored by developers alone
